title: Conjecture 1 (Plummer-Toft Conjecture)
statement_text: Every 3-connected plane graph with maximum face size $\Delta^\star$ has a cyclic coloring with at most $\Delta^\star + 2$ colors.
context_text: Plummer and Toft conjectured in 1987 that 3-connectivity removes the tight examples (which have degree-2 vertices) that force the $\lfloor 3\Delta^\star/2 \rfloor$ bound of the general Cyclic Coloring Conjecture, allowing a much smaller bound of $\Delta^\star + 2$. Prior to this paper the conjecture had been established for $\Delta^\star \in \{3,4\}$ and for all $\Delta^\star \geq 18$, leaving a finite gap of open cases.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for $\Delta^\star = 16$ and $\Delta^\star = 17$ using the discharging method with computer-verified reducible configurations, closing two of the remaining open cases.
paper_title: Cyclic Coloring of Plane Graphs with Maximum Face Size 16 and 17
